ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific entry for salmon grain bowl with egg; FOODON_00002809 (grain bowl food product) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_description: Mixed dishes — grain-based composite dish with fish and egg
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers composite grain-based dishes; no exact code exists for salmon grain bowl with egg and four toppings. Closest parent match documented.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this composite customisable grain bowl dish.

main_ingredients: mixed grains (brown rice, quinoa, or similar)
assorted toppings (e.g. avocado, edamame, leafy greens, cucumber, cherry tomato, corn)
allergens: gluten (if grain base contains wheat or barley)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 400 g, representing a full grain bowl with a mixed grain base, a portion of salmon, one egg, and four vegetable or legume toppings, served without sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Salmon grain bowl with egg (4 toppings), no sauce — HPB Singapore Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this dish (cr_id F08010056). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this canonical record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for salmon and egg as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this composite dish.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — mixed grain bowl analogous estimate based on brown rice / quinoa composite
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for this specific composite dish. GI estimated at 48 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entries for brown rice (GI ~50) and quinoa (GI ~53), weighted by typical grain-base composition in health bowls. The presence of salmon protein, egg protein, dietary fibre (2.07 g/100g), and the absence of sauce further attenuates postprandial glucose response. GL per serving calculated as: GI (48) × carbohydrate per serving (33.7 g for 400 g portion) / 100 = 16.2.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 192 mg/100g; no added sauce; potassium ~210 mg/100g from salmon, egg, and vegetable toppings
hypertension.guidance: At 192 mg sodium per 100 g and an estimated serving sodium of approximately 768 mg, this dish is borderline for a single meal in a 1500–2000 mg/day sodium-restricted diet; patients with hypertension should confirm toppings are not pickled or brined and should avoid adding any table condiments.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (~48), moderate carbohydrate 8.43 g/100g, high protein 7.05 g/100g, dietary fibre 2.07 g/100g
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low glycaemic index (~48), combined protein from salmon and egg (7.05 g/100g), and dietary fibre (2.07 g/100g) support attenuated postprandial glucose response; patients with Type 2 Diabetes should select a whole-grain base (brown rice or quinoa) and monitor total carbohydrate load per serving, particularly if HbA1c is above target.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Omega-3 fatty acids from salmon; low saturated fat ~0.95 g/100g; no sauce eliminates hidden trans fats and excess sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Salmon provides EPA and DHA omega-3 fatty acids which are cardioprotective; the low saturated fat content (~0.95 g/100g) and absence of sauce make this dish suitable for cardiovascular disease management, provided toppings are not fried and the grain base is whole grain.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: EPA/DHA from salmon; MUFA and vitamin E from avocado toppings; antioxidants from leafy greens
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Salmon-derived EPA and DHA omega-3 fatty acids have documented anti-inflammatory effects relevant to rheumatoid arthritis; patients should ensure salmon is grilled or baked rather than fried, and monitor tolerance to nightshade toppings such as tomato or bell pepper.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Salmon grain bowl with egg (4 toppings), no sauce is a contemporary health-oriented composite dish popular in Singapore's café and health-food restaurant scene. It consists of a base of mixed grains — typically brown rice, quinoa, or a combination — topped with a portion of cooked salmon (grilled or baked), one whole egg (boiled, poached, or soft-cooked), and four customer-selected toppings such as avocado, edamame, cucumber, cherry tomatoes, corn, leafy greens, or pickled vegetables. The dish is served without any sauce, which significantly reduces sodium and added-sugar content compared to dressed variants. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 99 kcal, 7.05 g protein, 4.07 g fat, 8.43 g carbohydrate, 2.07 g dietary fibre, and 192 mg sodium. The protein is high-quality, derived from both salmon (rich in EPA and DHA omega-3 fatty acids) and egg (complete amino acid profile). The fibre content from the grain base and vegetable toppings supports satiety and glycaemic modulation. The estimated glycaemic index of approximately 48 places this dish in the low-GI category, making it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or weight. The absence of sauce eliminates the primary source of hidden sodium and refined sugar typical of grain bowl variants. This dish is appropriate as a lunch or dinner meal and is widely available at health-food cafés and grain bowl chains across Singapore.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F08010056) with a reported confidence of 0.95. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, and cholesterol are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for salmon and egg and are consistent with the HPB composite values. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using brown rice and quinoa reference values.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (0.95 g/100g), monounsaturated fat (1.42 g/100g), polyunsaturated_fat_g (1.38 g/100g), potassium (210 mg/100g), and cholesterol (62 mg/100g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for Atlantic salmon (NDB 15076) and whole egg (NDB 01123), weighted by typical dish composition, as the HPB Foodo record does not separately report these values. 2. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and moisture data for this specific composite customisable dish are not reported in the HPB Foodo database, and component-level estimation for a four-topping variable dish would introduce unacceptable uncertainty; USDA FoodData Central does not have an exact match for this composite. 3. GI of 48 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific dish. 4. Halal status is assumed based on typical preparation at Singapore health-food establishments; individual outlets should be verified. 5. The four toppings are variable by customer choice; nutrient values reflect the HPB canonical average across typical topping combinations. 6. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed negligible for grilled salmon, egg, and whole-grain and vegetable components per USDA FoodData Central.
